content.problemStatement:
You are given a directed tree TTT containing NNN vertices, rooted at vertex 111. Each edge of the tree is directed from parent to child. The iii-th node initially contains a single token with label iii.

In one operation you do the following:


Select **any** edge of TTT, call it e = u → ve\:=\:u\:\rightarrow\:ve=u→v.
Move **all** tokens accumulated on uuu till now to vvv.
Delete eee from TTT.
You perform the above operation as long as TTT contains atleast 111 edge.

Find the number of different configurations of tokens on vertices that you can get in the end, modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

Two configurations are said to be different if for some vertex uuu, the set of tokens on it is different between the 222 configurations.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer QQQ, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN —  the number of vertices in the tree.
The next line contains (N−1)(N-1)(N−1) space-separated integers p2,p3,…,pn(1≤pi<i),p_2,p_3,\ldots,p_n (1\leq p_i \lt i),p2​,p3​,…,pn​(1≤pi​<i), where pip_ipi​ is the parent of iii in the tree.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of different final configurations, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: 1≤pi<i1 \leq p_i \lt i1≤pi​<i for each 2≤i≤N2 \leq i \leq N2≤i≤N.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5⋅1055\cdot 10^55⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Consider the below 444 orderings of removing edges from the tree and the end configuration of tokens.

1 → 51\:\rightarrow\:51→5, 2 → 42\:\rightarrow\:42→4, 1 → 21\:\rightarrow\:21→2, 2 → 32\:\rightarrow\:32→3

Node 3 has token 3.
Node 4 has tokens 2 and 4.
Node 5 has tokens 1 and 5.


1 → 21\:\rightarrow\:21→2, 1 → 51\:\rightarrow\:51→5, 2 → 32\:\rightarrow\:32→3, 2 → 42\:\rightarrow\:42→4

Node 3 has tokens 1,2 and 3.
Node 4 has token 4.
Node 5 has token 5.


2 → 32\:\rightarrow\:32→3, 1 → 21\:\rightarrow\:21→2, 1 → 51\:\rightarrow\:51→5, 2 → 42\:\rightarrow\:42→4

Node 3 has tokens 2 and 3.
Node 4 has tokens 1 and 4.
Node 5 has token 5.


2 → 42\:\rightarrow\:42→4, 2 → 32\:\rightarrow\:32→3, 1 → 51\:\rightarrow\:51→5, 1 → 21\:\rightarrow\:21→2

Node 3 has token 3.
Node 4 has tokens 2 and 4.
Node 5 has tokens 1 and 5.



Note that the first and fourth were different orderings of removing edges and even had different intermediate configurations, but the final configuration was same.
It can be proven that over all 242424 orderings of removing edges, only 888 different final configurations can be obtained.
